description: Tracks sent emails pending follow-up. One row per To-line recipient per sent message. The Sent Items Tracker flow creates rows; the Response Detection flow checks for replies and triggers nudges.

alternateKeys.purpose: Composite alternate key enabling safe Upsert. Prevents duplicate rows when the same sent email is processed more than once (e.g., Power Automate retry). One row per recipient per message.

columns.description: Graph message ID of the sent email. Part of composite alternate key. May be updated if the message is moved (Graph move invalidates the original ID).
Graph conversationId — primary mechanism for reply matching. Shared by all messages in the same thread.
Stored In-Reply-To and References headers from the sent email. Used as fallback matching when conversationId is unreliable (e.g., recipient changes subject).
When the email was sent. Used to calculate FollowUpDate and to scope the Graph reply search (only look for replies after this date).

Email address of the To-line recipient being tracked. Part of composite alternate key. One row per recipient per message. Reduced to 250 to keep composite key (200+250)×2=900 bytes at the SQL Server index limit.

Classification of the recipient: 'Internal' (same domain), 'External' (different domain), 'Priority' (on priority contact list), or 'General' (default). Determines which NudgeConfiguration column to use for follow-up delay.
Email subject line. Used as the primary column display name and in the nudge Adaptive Card.
Calculated: cr_sentdatetime + configured business days (from NudgeConfiguration). The Response Detection flow queries rows where this date is in the past.
Whether a reply from this specific recipient has been detected via Graph API. Set to true by the Response Detection flow.
Whether the follow-up nudge Adaptive Card has been delivered to the user. Set to true after successful Teams card delivery.
Whether the user dismissed this nudge via the Adaptive Card 'Dismiss' button. Prevents further nudging for this recipient on this message.

Timestamp of the last time the Response Detection flow checked this row for a reply. Used as a race condition guard — the flow re-queries the row before sending a nudge to confirm the state hasn't changed.

notes.cardinality: One row per To-line recipient per sent message. CC recipients are excluded. Example: an email to 3 To-line recipients creates 3 rows, all sharing the same cr_sourcesignalid and cr_conversationid.
notes.state_transitions: Three booleans (cr_responsereceived, cr_nudgesent, cr_dismissedbyuser) create 8 possible states. Valid states: Awaiting (F/F/F), Nudged (F/T/F), Replied (T/F/F), Replied-After-Nudge (T/T/F), Dismissed (F/F/T), Dismissed-After-Nudge (F/T/T). Invalid: any state where cr_responsereceived=true AND cr_dismissedbyuser=true (prevent in flow logic).
notes.data_retention: A weekly scheduled cleanup flow should delete rows older than 90 days where cr_responsereceived=true OR cr_dismissedbyuser=true.
notes.reply_matching: Primary: match Graph messages by cr_conversationid where from.emailAddress.address equals cr_recipientemail and receivedDateTime > cr_sentdatetime. Fallback: match via In-Reply-To/References headers in cr_internetmessageheaders.
notes.security: UserOwned table with ownership-based RLS. Users can only read/write their own rows.
notes.system_columns: Standard Dataverse system columns (ownerid, createdon, modifiedon, statecode, statuscode) are created automatically.
